title: Design HashMap
pattern: Design â€” Array of Buckets with Chaining
coreIdea: Implement a hashmap from scratch using an array of fixed size as buckets, where each bucket holds a list of (key, value) pairs, resolving collisions by chaining (multiple entries in the same bucket).
recognitionCues: asks to implement a hashmap/dictionary without using the language's built-in map
tests understanding of hashing and collision resolution
approach: Use an array of a fixed size (e.g., 1000) of lists (buckets). Compute bucket index via key % array size (a simple hash function). Put: check if key exists in that bucket's list and update, else append new pair. Get: linear scan the bucket's list for the key. Remove: linear scan and remove matching pair.
timeComplexity: O(1) average per operation, O(n) worst case with many collisions
